architecture.summary: PlaM is a training-free model merging framework that diagnoses multimodal large language models (MLLMs) via layer-wise vision token masking to identify three-stage patterns (early-modal separation, mid-modal alignment, late-modal degradation), then selectively injects base language model parameters into MLLM layers at identified degradation stages to restore text reasoning capability while preserving multimodal performance.

biblio.title: PlaM: Training-Free Plateau-Guided Model Merging for Better Visual Grounding in MLLMs

claims.statement: Multimodal instruction fine-tuning paradoxically degrades the text reasoning capability inherited from base language models, undermining multimodal performance.
Layer-wise vision token masking reveals a common three-stage pattern in multimodal large language models: early-modal separation, mid-modal alignment, and late-modal degradation.
The plateau-guided model merging method selectively injects base language model parameters into MLLMs to mitigate text reasoning degradation.
Experimental results based on five MLLMs on nine benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of the method.
Attention-based analysis reveals that merging shifts attention from diffuse, scattered patterns to focused localization on task-relevant visual regions.
